key_drivers: Falling and historically low real interest rates
reasoning: The indicators provide a confluent bullish signal for equities. The US Treasury yield curve is upward sloping (normal) and rising, which signifies market expectations of future economic expansion [57205ed3e4b4, 98b8f9c4befb]. Simultaneously, the 10-year real interest rate is at the 3rd percentile (very low) and falling; according to expert rules, this increases liquidity, reduces volatility in risk assets, and supports expansionary GDP [99ad6cdde172, 2328dabf508c, 239bc87b8f03]. Furthermore, real rates near zero typically allow equity indices to command higher PE valuation multiples [5e068f4bde46]. This is corroborated by the VIX index, which is in a normal regime at the 34th percentile, consistent with the suppressed volatility associated with very low real rates [0826c79434b2].
